posts.text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming! 🚀

comments.text: Hi Frank Li I think I can help them. Can you please share those people list on pathey@1911solutions.com

resharedPost.text:
As everyone is reflecting on the BlackBerry device end of life today, I had to join the bandwagon with a reflection and love post. I feel so incredibly blessed to have been a part of this roller coaster ride from the early days of the Inter@ctive Pager to the incredible BB10 launches and almost everything in between. But when I reflect on the BlackBerry days, I think of the people the most. #TeamBlackBerry really was incredible.

1/ The Berry Addiction: I was BlackBerry obsessed right from the start. Somewhere in this house, I still have the cardboard mockup that Mike L gave me in the 90s. I obsessed over the red flashing light (the spark too *), and never have I been more accurate and productive than when I used my BlackBerry. I miss it all the time.

2/ The People: I had the good fortune of working for all of the BlackBerry CEOs during my time at BlackBerry – 3 CEO transitions, new boards and new executive teams during that time. Incredible to think about it now. I’ve also had the good fortune of working with some of the most talented, hardest working, passionate, and skilled people of my career at BlackBerry. I’ve loved them all. If someone ever asks for a reference about a #TeamBlackBerry member, I am always happy to give it because no group was as hard working, talented, resilient, and worked at every budget level and with every hurdle you could imagine. They hustled like crazy and led with their hearts. We asked them to do so much and they did that and then some. I’ll be forever grateful.

3/ The Future: Quoting Ina Fried in Axios today: BlackBerry no longer manufactures phones, but it makes more than $1 Billion in revenue per year selling things like security software, an operating system widely used in car's and its patent licenses. Chances are, your cars telemetry system is running BlackBerry software. So, if you’ve gotten past your potential PTSD and caught up on your rest, I hope that you take some pride in the fact that you helped to pivot an incredible brand into something new – that has survived to fight another day.

I am forever changed by my BlackBerry experience, for the many gifts and blessings that our Canadian experience had on our lives and for our family. For the lifelong friendships we’ve created and for the many learnings of this shared experience, I say THANK YOU, diehard users. THANK YOU, #TeamBlackBerry.

comments.text:
Hey Heidi !
👈 see the Elite spark on my PP ? 😉

And this post is why I love my #BlackBerry , always in Motion. Always resilient. Its death was greatly exaggerated in 2013 , it's 2022 and the company has reinvented itself , resilient as ever. In an App and Camera world , there is not much to distinguish a phone anymore. But the team at BlackBerry, particular those that worked on the Passport. Thank you #TeamBlackBerry. Best phone I owned , ergonomics , quality build and speaker and sound quality second to none. Here's to the Resilient Ones. ❤

resharedPost.text:
This needs to be reiterated a lot 

To all the students out there,

You are doing absolutely well, you are doing great at your own level, and it's just that the right time for you to achieve your dream goals is yet to come.

If you are in your first/second year, you will also get a chance to sit for your dream company through on/off campus placements/internship in the coming years. Buddy you are doing absolutely great, and soon you will achieve your dreams.

You couldn't bag that dream internship in your third year, no worries you will do it in your final year placements. Keep working and learning on your current internship/ semester and move towards your goal. Buddy you are doing absolutely great, and soon you will achieve your dreams

You are in your final year and couldn't get your dream company that's absolutely okay, you will bag it in first year of career, just like I did. Again buddy you are doing absolutely great, and soon you will achieve your dreams.

Like I wrote this before, your career doesn't need to be according to someone else's time line.

Pat your back you are doing absolutely great.
 #career #learning

posts.text: Just finished Start With Why. Really good book that my friend Seif reccomended. Biggest take away: "People don't buy what you do people buy why you do it"

posts.text:
I founded Avocadocore 2 years ago to build an adaptive learning platform that takes the luck out of education and give everyone an equal opportunity at success. Since then our software has been ran in multiple courses in universities with hundreds of students each semester. 

This could only been possible because of the awesome team of engineers, programmers, academic specialists, and business analysts working together towards our common vision. As demand for our software increases at Western, UofT, and Fanshaw, we will need your expertise and help to reach our next major milestones. 

If getting involved in working for a friendly and professional company whose goal is towards better education interests you please check out the poster below and apply!

www.avocadocore.com/apply

Looking forward to meeting you,
Frank
